control_rationale: Good concrete Java patch surface with outbox, retry scheduling, dead-lettering, and lag/backlog metrics. However it indexes directly in the request path and enqueues a snapshot on failure, which can still reapply stale data later and can regress docs after newer updates unless ordering/version enforcement is added. Source-of-truth intent is noted, but failure handling and idempotency are incomplete relative to derived-data reconciliation requirements.
treatment_rationale: Strongly models search as derived state and replaces inline dual-write with an outbox-backed durable replay path, including bounded eventual consistency and explicit recovery semantics. It addresses key failure modes (crash, indexing outage, backlog) and adds observability plus tests/checks. Idempotency/retry safety is mostly present but less concrete on backoff/dead-letter strategy and exact worker concurrency safety.
judge_rationale: Response A is better aligned with DDIA-derived-state recovery goals because it avoids the request-path indexing write as the recovery mechanism and makes replay from durable DB intent the primary path, while including reconciliation-minded verification. Response B is more concrete but has a real correctness gap (possible stale replay overwrite) and weaker observability/testing completeness, so it is lower overall.
